channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Media's Use of 'Trial of the Century' Label in Duterte Impeachment Case
item.description: The article examines how media outlets have framed Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial as a 'trial of the century,' comparing it to past high-profile cases. It highlights the tendency of j...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Warns Regional Tensions Could Impact Pakistan's Economy
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has cautioned that escalating tensions in the Middle East, particularly the recent US-Iran clashes, could negatively affect Pakistan's economy. During a meeting to assess...

item.title: Creating a Home Spa: Simple Tips for Relaxation
item.description: The article explores how to transform your bathroom into a personal spa sanctuary for relaxation and self-care. Author Chantel Mila emphasizes that creating a spa-like atmosphere at home doesn't requi...

item.title: Senior Traditional Leader Nkosi Mpumalanga Gwadiso Dies Suddenly in Eastern Cape
item.description: Nkosi Mpumalanga Gwadiso, 63, the chair of the Eastern Cape House of Traditional and Khoisan Leaders, died suddenly in his sleep at his home in Ngqeleni. His death was confirmed by family members and ...

item.title: UK government rejects mandatory breathalysers in cars, prioritises other safety measures
item.description: The UK government has decided not to make breathalysers (alcolocks) mandatory in new cars, despite considering them as a potential tool to combat drink-driving. Following a consultation that closed in...

item.title: Crowd congestion during Puri Rath Yatra leads to multiple cases of fainting among devotees
item.description: During the annual Puri Rath Yatra festival, a significant number of devotees experienced health issues due to overcrowding and lack of proper crowd management. Reports indicate that several individual...

item.title: Advocates Push for Safer Drug Supply to Combat Overdose Crisis in Manitoba
item.description: The article highlights the growing concern over opioid-related deaths in Manitoba, where advocates are urging policymakers to consider safer drug supply programs as a potential solution. Families of t...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Port achieves record cargo growth of 6 million tonnes
item.description: Dar es Salaam Port has set a new record by handling 33.7 million tonnes of cargo in the 2025/26 financial year, a 6 million tonne increase from the previous year's 27.7 million tonnes. This growth, at...

item.title: Analysis of Trump's Financial and Legal Accounts
item.description: The article examines the financial and legal accounts associated with Donald Trump, focusing on controversies surrounding his business dealings, tax practices, and legal battles. It highlights key cas...

item.title: TV Networks Hesitant to Air Trump's Speech Amid Calls for Censorship
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's planned prime-time address faces uncertainty as major television networks have not confirmed they will broadcast his speech. The Daily Beast reports that ABC, CBS, NBC, F...

item.title: EU Highlights Compliance Issues in Pakistan's GSP+ Framework, Calls for Addressing Shortcomings
item.description: The European Union has raised concerns about Pakistan's compliance with its GSP+ commitments, emphasizing the need for Islamabad to address persistent shortcomings to retain access to the preferential...

item.title: NGO Urges Swift Justice for Convicted Child Abuser in South Africa
item.description: An NGO focused on combating child abuse has called for the immediate sentencing of Iain Ware, an 86-year-old convicted sex offender, following the postponement of his pre-sentencing trial in the Wynbe...

item.title: South Air Launches Inaugural Flights from Karachi to Quetta and Islamabad, Enhancing Domestic Connectivity
item.description: South Air, a new private airline, has commenced its inaugural flights from Karachi to Quetta via Turbat and to Islamabad via Bahawalpur, as confirmed by the Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA). The PAA ...

item.title: Aina secures $5.5M to develop AI control devices beyond simple recording
item.description: Aina, a startup founded by Apoorv Shankar—a former Ultrahuman hardware VP—has raised $5.5 million in funding led by Redstart Labs and 360 ONE. The investment comes as the company aims to create hardwa...

item.title: New York Times Focus Group Finds Some Young Trump Voters Dissatisfied With Economy and Foreign Policy
item.description: The article reports on a New York Times focus group involving 10 voters between the ages of 20 and 25, most of whom supported Donald Trump in the 2024 presidential election. While the participants wer...

item.title: Alego Usonga MP Samuel Atandi Donates KSh 500k to Tony Ndiema's Burial
item.description: MP Samuel Atandi, representing Alego Usonga, donated KSh 500,000 towards the burial of late Ohangla singer Tony Ndiema during a candlelight vigil held on July 15, 2026. The event took place at a club ...

item.title: Happy Farm in Tarlac: A Family-Friendly Tropical Retreat
item.description: The article highlights Happy Farm in the Tropics, a resort located in Santa Ignacia, Tarlac, designed as a family-friendly destination. It emphasizes the farm's vibrant atmosphere, ideal for families ...

item.title: Fatal bus crash on Abuja-Kaduna highway claims 11 lives
item.description: A commercial bus carrying 16 passengers collided with a stationary truck on the Abuja-Kaduna highway, killing 11 people and injuring five others. The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) confirmed the inc...

item.title: Canada Considers Permanent Daylight Saving Time After U.S. Bill Passes
item.description: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a bill to end seasonal time changes, sparking debate about Canada's potential follow suit. While provinces like Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia hav...

item.title: Arizona County Reaches Election Management Agreement Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Election officials in Arizona's most populous county, Maricopa, have reached a settlement to resolve a months-long legal battle over election oversight. Republican county recorder Justin Heap and the ...
